tool.name: MMseqs2 easy-linsearch
tool.description: for fast homology search scaling linearly
tool.command:
#set $query_ext = str($query_fasta.ext).replace('sanger', '').replace('illumina', '') ln -s '$query_fasta' 'query.${query_ext}' && #if str($target_source.target_source_select) == "history" #set $target_ext = str($target_source.target_fasta.ext).replace('sanger', '').replace('illumina', '') ln -s '$target_source.target_fasta' 'target.${target_ext}' && #end if
mmseqs easy-linsearch 'query.${query_ext}' #if str($target_source.target_source_select) == "history" 'target.${target_ext}' #else '$target_source.mmseqs2_db_select.fields.path'/database #end if 'search_results' 'tmp'
#if str($alph_type.dbtype) == "1" --comp-bias-corr-scale $alph_type.comp_bias_corr_scale --kmer-per-seq-scale $alph_type.kmer_per_seq_scale #elif str($alph_type.dbtype) == "2" --zdrop $alph_type.zdrop --kmer-per-seq-scale $alph_type.kmer_per_seq_scale --adjust-kmer-len $alph_type.adjust_kmer_len #end if
## Pre-filter options (linsearch accepts fewer prefilter params than search) --add-self-matches $prefilter.add_self_matches --mask $prefilter.mask --mask-prob $prefilter.mask_prob --mask-lower-case $prefilter.mask_lower_case --mask-n-repeat $prefilter.mask_n_repeat
## Align options @CMD_ALIGN@ @CMD_SEARCH_ALIGN@
## K-mer matcher options (linsearch subset) --kmer-per-seq $kmermatcher.kmer_per_seq
## Misc options --id-offset $misc.id_offset
## Output format @CMD_FORMAT_OUTPUT@
## Search type --search-type $search_type
## Common options @CMD_THREADS@ --max-seq-len $common.max_seq_len

param.name: query_fasta
param.label: Query FASTA/Q file
param.help: Protein or nucleotide sequences to search. The search type is auto-detected from the input.

param.label: Scale k-mer per sequence based on sequence length
param.help: Scales the number of k-mers selected per sequence based on its length. Higher values increase sensitivity.

param.label: Search type
param.help: Controls the alignment algorithm. Auto detects from input sequences. See the help section below for details on each mode and cross-type searches.

param.label: Artificially add entries of queries with themselves (for clustering)
param.help: Useful when clustering to ensure each sequence matches itself.

param.label: Mask sequences in k-mer stage
param.help: Low complexity regions can cause spurious hits. Masking filters them during k-mer matching.

param.label: Mask sequences if probability is above threshold
param.help: Regions with compositional bias probability above this threshold are masked.

param.label: Mask lower case letters
param.help: Controls whether lower case letters in input sequences are treated as masked regions.

param.label: Repeat letters that occur > threshold in a row
param.help: Mask stretches of N characters longer than this threshold. Set 0 to disable.

param.label: k-mers per sequence
param.help: More k-mers per sequence increases sensitivity but uses more memory.

param.label: Numeric IDs in index file are offset by this value
param.help: Add this offset to all numeric sequence IDs in the output.

tool.help:
@MMSEQS2_LOGO@
**About this tool**
``easy-linsearch`` performs **linear-time homology searches** using exact k-mer matching. It is the faster alternative to **easy-search**: use it when searching very large databases and high-identity hits are expected. For finding remote homologs or when maximum sensitivity is needed, use **easy-search** with ``-s 7.0`` instead.
**How it works**
Unlike easy-search (which uses scored k-mer prefiltering with tunable sensitivity), easy-linsearch uses the same exact k-mer matching approach as Linclust:
1. Selects representative k-mers from both query and target sequences 2. Matches k-mers exactly (no scoring threshold) 3. Aligns candidate pairs via Smith-Waterman
This makes prefiltering O(n) in database size, but limits sensitivity for detecting divergent homologs. The target database should be indexed with ``createlinindex`` for optimal performance when searching repeatedly.
**Example usage**
*Basic fast search* Upload your query and target FASTA files and run with defaults. easy-linsearch is ideal when your database is very large and you expect high-identity hits (e.g., > 70% identity).
*High-identity filtering* Set **Minimum sequence identity** to ``0.9`` and **Minimum coverage** to ``0.8`` to find only near-identical matches quickly.
*Nucleotide search* Set **Search type** to **Nucleotide** for nucleotide-vs-nucleotide search without translation.
**easy-linsearch vs easy-search**
- **easy-linsearch**: Linear-time prefiltering, faster for large databases, best for high-identity searches. - **easy-search**: Scored k-mer prefiltering with tunable sensitivity (``-s`` parameter), better for detecting remote homologs, approaches BLAST sensitivity at ``-s 7.0``.
**Search types**
The search type is auto-detected from input sequences but can be overridden:
- **Auto** (default): MMseqs2 detects the appropriate mode from input sequences - **Amino acid**: protein vs protein search (BLASTP equivalent) - **Translated**: nucleotide query vs protein target with 6-frame translation (BLASTX equivalent), or protein query vs nucleotide target (TBLASTN equivalent) - **Nucleotide**: nucleotide vs nucleotide search without translation (BLASTN equivalent) - **Translated nucleotide alignment**: both query and target are translated (TBLASTX equivalent)
For **cross-type searches** (e.g., nucleotide query against a protein database), leave *Input data type* on **Automatic** and set *Search type* to **Translated**. The *Input data type* (``--dbtype``) parameter applies to both query and target in easy-* commands, so setting it to Nucleotides or Amino acid forces both sides to that type.
**Key parameters**
- **E-value (-e)**: Maximum E-value threshold for reporting hits (default 0.001). - **Coverage (-c, --cov-mode)**: Minimum alignment coverage and coverage calculation mode. - **K-mers per sequence (--kmer-per-seq-scale)**: Scales the number of k-mers selected per sequence based on sequence length. - **Output columns (--format-output)**: Select specific columns for BLAST-tab output.
**Outputs**
- **BLAST-TAB** (default): Tab-separated table with 12 columns matching BLAST ``-outfmt 6`` - **BLAST-TAB with lengths**: Adds query and target sequence length columns - **BLAST-TAB with headers**: Includes a column header line - **SAM**: Standard alignment format compatible with samtools - **Pretty HTML**: Visual alignment display for interactive browsing
-----
@HELP_OVERVIEW@ @HELP_FOOTER@
